title: Deputy Prime Minister; Minister of Economy and Finance

leader.name: Aun Pornmoniroth
leader.position: Deputy Prime Minister; Minister of Economy and Finance
leader.purpose: Stub profile — to be populated by extractor pipeline

growth_theory.assessed_position: Macroeconomic stability as the precondition for everything else. Pornmoniroth's growth model is explicitly fiscal-first: budget discipline, low inflation, revenue mobilisation, and a stable riel environment create the platform on which FDI and export manufacturing can operate. He is not an industrial strategist — he is a fiscal architect. His stated priorities when managing the economy are: GDP gr
growth_theory.primary_quote: For 2024, Cambodia's economy is forecast to achieve a growth rate of around 6.6 percent, which will increase the GDP to 35.16 billion US dollars.

_evidence.quote: For 2024, Cambodia's economy is forecast to achieve a growth rate of around 6.6 percent, which will increase the GDP to 35.16 billion US dollars.

risk_tolerance.assessed_position: Pornmoniroth has never made a bold unilateral bet. His 12-year tenure at MEF is marked by incremental improvement within a stable macro framework. He projected 6.6% growth publicly while IMF/ADB projected 5.3–5.4%, but this is a communications risk, not a policy risk. His actual fiscal management has been conservative: deficit targets maintained, debt levels managed, reserves built. The one area o
subfields: Assessment: LOW-MEDIUM — Institutionally cautious, fiscally orthodox

time_horizon.assessed_position: Pornmoniroth chairs the Supreme National Economic Council and is involved in the Pentagonal Strategy's 25-year framing, but his actual decision-making operates on a 1–3 year fiscal cycle. Budget laws are his primary policy instrument. The 2030 digital economy strategy is his longest-horizon published document. His public framing consistently references Cambodia's 2030 upper-middle-income target, b

dependency_assumptions.assessed_position: 1. Garment and travel goods exports sustain or grow: industrial sector projected to grow 8.5% in 2024, driven by garment and construction. If US tariffs (49% shock in 2025) had not been negotiated down, this assumption would have failed. 2. subfields: Load-bearing assumptions in the fiscal model

institution_vs_relationship.assessed_position: More institutionally oriented than most of his cabinet peers. MEF has built genuine inter-agency coordination mechanisms (NCFS, CEFP, Digital Economy and Business Committee). The business registry digitisation, CamDX platform, and online investment approval system are real institutional outputs — they reduce personal interface with government by design. However, his family networks (marriage into

global_positioning_logic.assessed_position: Pornmoniroth chairs the AIIB board, coordinates with IMF and World Bank, and engages with ADB as Cambodia's key multilateral partners. His positioning theory is that multilateral engagement builds the credibility that sustains FDI, credit ratings, and concessional financing access. He is not ideologically anti-West or anti-China — he is pro-stability. His US tariff response (rapid offer to cut 19
subfields: Theory: Multilateral engagement as fiscal stability insurance
Overall: MEDIUM-HIGH on fiscal mechanics; LOW on structural transformation
